title: [BUG] date parsing doesn't work in streaming mode
author: yocontra

comments.author: yocontra
comments.body:
Dump of the XML for this worksheet from `utils/parse-sax.js`:

```xml
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<worksheet xmlns="http://schemas.openxmlformats.org/spreadsheetml/2006/main" xmlns:r="http://schemas.openxmlformats.org/officeDocument/2006/relationships">
  <sheetPr filterMode="false">
    <pageSetUpPr fitToPage="false" />
  </sheetPr>
  <dimension ref="A1:D65536" />
  <sheetViews>
    <sheetView windowProtection="false" showFormulas="false" showGridLines="true" showRowColHeaders="true" showZeros="true" rightToLeft="false" tabSelected="true" showOutlineSymbols="true" defaultGridColor="true" view="normal" topLeftCell="A1" colorId="64" zoomScale="100" zoomScaleNormal="100" zoomScalePageLayoutView="60" workbookViewId="0">
      <selection pane="topLeft" activeCell="A1" activeCellId="0" sqref="A1" />
    </sheetView>
  </sheetViews>
  <sheetFormatPr defaultRowHeight="15.75" />
  <cols>
    <col collapsed="false" hidden="false" max="256" min="1" style="0" width="14.4285714285714" />
    <col collapsed="false" hidden="false" max="1025" min="257" style="0" width="11.5204081632653" />
  </cols>
  <sheetData>
    <row r="1" customFormat="false" ht="15.75" hidden="false" customHeight="false" outlineLevel="0" collapsed="false">
      <c r="A1" s="1" t="s">
        <v>0</v>
      </c>
      <c r="B1" s="1" t="s">
        <v>1</v>
      </c>
      <c r="C1" s="1" t="s">
        <v>2</v>
      </c>
      <c r="D1" s="1" t="s">
        <v>3</v>
      </c>
    </row>
    <row r="2" customFormat="false" ht="15.1" hidden="false" customHeight="false" outlineLevel="0" collapsed="false">
      <c r="A2" s="1" t="s">
        <v>4</v>
      </c>
      <c r="B2" s="2" t="n">
        <v>42774</v>
      </c>
      <c r="C2" s="1" t="n">
        <v>100</v>
      </c>
      <c r="D2" s="1" t="n">
        <v>111</v>
      </c>
    </row>
    <row r="3" customFormat="false" ht="15.1" hidden="false" customHeight="false" outlineLevel="0" collapsed="false">
      <c r="A3" s="1" t="s">
        <v>5</v>
      </c>
      <c r="B3" s="2" t="n">
        <v>42774</v>
      </c>
      <c r="C3" s="1" t="n">
        <v>300</v>
      </c>
      <c r="D3" s="1" t="n">
        <v>222</v>
      </c>
    </row>
    <row r="4" customFormat="false" ht="15.1" hidden="false" customHeight="false" outlineLevel="0" collapsed="false">
      <c r="A4" s="1" t="s">
        <v>6</v>
      </c>
      <c r="B4" s="2" t="n">
        <v>42774</v>
      </c>
      <c r="C4" s="1" t="n">
        <v>4</v>
      </c>
      <c r="D4" s="1" t="n">
        <v>333</v>
      </c>
    </row>
    <row r="5" customFormat="false" ht="15.1" hidden="false" customHeight="false" outlineLevel="0" collapsed="false">
      <c r="A5" s="1" t="s">
        <v>7</v>
      </c>
      <c r="B5" s="2" t="n">
        <v>42774</v>
      </c>
      <c r="C5" s="1" t="n">
        <v>53</v>
      </c>
      <c r="D5" s="1" t="n">
        <v>444</v>
      </c>
    </row>
    <row r="1048576" customFormat="false" ht="15.75" hidden="false" customHeight="true" outlineLevel="0" collapsed="false" />
  </sheetData>
  <printOptions headings="false" gridLines="false" gridLinesSet="true" horizontalCentered="false" verticalCentered="false" />
  <pageMargins left="0.7875" right="0.7875" top="1.025" bottom="1.025" header="0.7875" footer="0.7875" />
  <pageSetup paperSize="9" scale="100" firstPageNumber="1" fitToWidth="1" fitToHeight="1" pageOrder="downThenOver" orientation="portrait" usePrinterDefaults="false" blackAndWhite="false" draft="false" cellComments="none" useFirstPageNumber="true" horizontalDpi="300" verticalDpi="300" copies="1" />
  <headerFooter differentFirst="false" differentOddEven="false">
    <oddHeader>&C&A</oddHeader>
    <oddFooter>&CPage &P</oddFooter>
  </headerFooter>
</worksheet>
```

So it does appear that the file does have column B with `s="2"` which this library understands as a general number, but Excel renders as a date - at a loss for how to debug this any further.

comments.author: coolwhite520
comments.body: I Have the same question. Who can help me to solve this bug???

comments.author: coolwhite520
comments.body: This problem has been bothering me. I hope the team can fix this bug.

comments.author: rheidari
yocontra
comments.body: @rheidari That would explain a lot, I looked at the docs and couldn't figure out why the behavior looked correct in the code but in practice was broken. I'll try it out and see if that fixes it.

comments.author: yocontra
comments.body: Quick note @rheidari - didn't seem to help, don't think the defaults are the issue unless the merging is broken. I'll dig into it more today.

comments.author: dxhuy
comments.body:
> I had this issue as well, and it was due to me ignoring styles. The streaming reader has a second options parameter `WorkbookStreamReaderOptions`. The docs says it defaults to cache, but according to the type definition it defaults to ignore. https://github.com/exceljs/exceljs#streaming-xlsx-readercontents
> 
> ```js
> new Excel.stream.xlsx.WorkbookReader(filePath, {
> 	entries: 'emit',
> 	sharedStrings: 'cache',
> 	hyperlinks: 'cache',
> 	styles: 'cache',
> 	worksheets: 'emit',
> });
> ```

Thanks a lot. I got the same issue, after changing the second option as your comment, it parsed the date type correctly.

comments.author: vksonagara1996
comments.body:
> I had this issue as well, and it was due to me ignoring styles. The streaming reader has a second options parameter `WorkbookStreamReaderOptions`. The docs says it defaults to cache, but according to the type definition it defaults to ignore. https://github.com/exceljs/exceljs#streaming-xlsx-readercontents
> 
> ```js
> new Excel.stream.xlsx.WorkbookReader(filePath, {
> 	entries: 'emit',
> 	sharedStrings: 'cache',
> 	hyperlinks: 'cache',
> 	styles: 'cache',
> 	worksheets: 'emit',
> });
> ```

Thanks, after using these options, my issue got fixed!

comments.author: vksonagara1996
comments.body: And yeah by default it is ignoring styles, but in documentation it says by default it is "cache".

comments.author: yocontra
comments.body: I can also confirm the issue is fixed with those options - I think the solution here is to adjust the code default value to match the README, I can send a PR for it later today

comments.author: yocontra
comments.body:
Quick update: There is a race condition with the streaming parser, depending on the ordering of the files in the excel zip it will start parsing the worksheet before the styles have been parsed and dates will just come in as numbers. 

Specifically I've fixed it with this change: https://github.com/contra/exceljs/commit/457b0b35d54b5eea4c72d3c9fb53318e817083a0

I've run into a handful of race conditions, stalls and other issues with the streaming parser - once I have everything working perfectly I'll send a PR.

comments.author: pavitran10
comments.body: I just came through this solution. It is worked for me. But, when I checked date that created by formula still I am getting date as serial, how I get normal cell date without setting this option. How make work for also date that created by formula?
